Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Hmm, the user has provided a detailed description of a multinational supply chain for smartphone manufacturing and asked for 20 thought-provoking, open-ended questions. The questions should aim to uncover insights, potential issues, improvement strategies, and decision implications.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First, I need to ensure the questions are truly open-ended and thought-provoking, not just superficial inquiries. They should encourage deep analysis of the process. The user emphasized no SQL queries, so Ill focus solely on qualitative questions.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The supply chain involves multiple components: RD, sourcing, manufacturing, logistics, distribution, and post-sales support. I should cover each area while also considering cross-cutting themes like risk management, sustainability, and technology integration.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For sourcing, questions about supplier diversification and geopolitical risks come to mind. Manufacturing raises points about automation and labor practices. Logistics involves trade-offs between cost and speed. Distribution needs questions about channel strategies and inventory optimization.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I also want to include questions about broader implications, like how data analytics can transform decision-making, or how regulations across regions create complexity. The questions should challenge assumptions and explore what if scenarios to help the user think beyond the obvious.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Ill structure them to flow logically from upstream to downstream, but the order isnt critical as long as theyre diverse and insightful. Let me draft them now, making sure each one prompts reflection rather than a simple yes/no answer.
